hawkish_phrases: some modest tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period was likely to be necessary
it will be necessary over coming months to increase Bank Rate in order to return CPI inflation sustainably to the 2% target
CPI inflation was now expected to peak at around 5% in April 2022
risks around the November Report projection for wage growth to ease were judged to be skewed to the upside
Two members...preferred an immediate increase in Bank Rate to 0.25%
dovish_phrases: there was value in waiting for additional information on near-term developments in the labour market
downside risks to demand could be accentuated by the impact of higher prices on households' real incomes
Current elevated global cost pressures were still most likely to prove transitory
the ability to loosen monetary policy in response to any significant negative demand shock in the future was to some degree constrained
UK GDP growth was expected to be relatively subdued...margin of spare capacity was expected to emerge
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.1% with two members dissenting for an immediate 15bp hike, while signaling that rate increases will be necessary in coming months if data aligns with projections showing inflation peaking at 5%.
